Fibroblasts are indeed the most abundant cells in the cell-rich zone because they actively contribute to the structure and maintenance of connective tissues, which includes important roles in reparative processes and matrix production. While the other cell types have specific functions (odontoblasts form dentin, ameloblasts create enamel), their numbers are generally lower compared to fibroblasts in most regions except those dedicated to hard tissue formation.
